Details: One study reported effective cyanide removal using ultraviolet (UV) irradiation combined with hydrogen peroxide with copper as catalyst [1359]. Initial cyanide concentration was 100 mg/L. The study showed cyanide removal was greater than 99 percent with a low-pressure UV lamp, 1200 mg/L hydrogen peroxide and 10 to 25 mg/L copper as catalyst within 8 to 14 minutes. UV and hydrogen peroxide without copper can also remove cyanide, but at a much lower rate. A 99 percent removal was reached in 45 minutes without copper compared to 8 to 14 minutes with copper.
